summary_text: The run is invalid for a minimal recoverable reason pattern: `tmux-session-missing-locally` is checked in `kernel-runtime-health.sh`, and when scheduler state is live (`running`/`continuity_degraded`) that maps to `state=invalid`, `lifecycle=blocked` in `derive_lifecycle_state`. So the safest path is to restore the tmux/session linkage first, then verify; only then terminalize if needed. Minimal safe recovery path (explicitly pinned to `~/.config/kernel`): - `RUN_ID='adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:42806:run:20260402T102832:10551'` - `export KERNEL_STATE_ROOT="$HOME/.config/kernel" KERNEL_SUBSTRATE_STATE_ROOT="$HOME/.config/kernel"` 1) Read-only precheck against intended state root: - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-state-paths.sh state-root` - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh status "$RUN_ID"` - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh snapshot --write` - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-claim.sh list` - `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh status` 2) Intended recovery command: - `bash scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh recover-run "$RUN_ID"` (equivalent to `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh recover "$RUN_ID"`) 3) Re-validate: - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh status "$RUN_ID"` - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh snapshot` If `recover-run` does not clear it, explicit close path: - Get identity: `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-claim.sh list --active-only | jq -r --arg r "$RUN_ID" '.[] | select(.run_id==$r) | .identity'` - If identity exists, set claim terminal: - `bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-claim.sh set-state --identity "<identity>" --state terminal --reason "manual-invalid-clear"` - Align scheduler lifecycle: - `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh scheduler-state terminal "manual-invalid-clear"` - Optional mirror of generic state transition: - `KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh transition terminal "manual-invalid-clear"` If there is a run workspace (for hard stop semantics), also drop a stop flag: - `WORKSPACE_DIR="$(KERNEL_RUN_ID="$RUN_ID" bash scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-workspace.sh path)"` - `[ -n "$WORKSPACE_DIR" ] && mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE_DIR" && : > "$WORKSPACE_DIR/stop"` No files were edited in this process.
